candidate.lever: Add a dispatch-layer BatchTrace direct kernel for single-input, single-equation I64 Add with a literal and resolved in_axes=1, producing the default out_axes=0 transposed layout without per-equation BatchTrace interpreter scaffolding.
candidate.decision: Source diff removed; no slower code kept.

behavior_proof.ordering: Candidate wrote output in the same default vmap out-axis row-major order: columns become leading batch lanes, rows remain inner order.
behavior_proof.tie_breaking: No comparisons or tie-breaking are introduced.
behavior_proof.floating_point: Accepted path was I64-only; no FP operations are introduced.
behavior_proof.rng: No RNG state is read or written.
behavior_proof.fallbacks: Only exact one-equation I64 add-literal Jaxprs with resolved in_axes=1 were accepted; all other cases fell back to existing dispatch.
behavior_proof.rch_tests: cargo test -p fj-dispatch test_in_axes_1 -- --nocapture passed on ts2: 3 passed, 0 failed.

next_primitive: Do not repeat direct axis-1 add kernels. Two rejected micro/dispatch kernels now point to a different attack: linalg-local QR/SVD/Eigh kernels when ownership clears, or a broader dispatch evidence/cache construction primitive with profiling that shows response scaffolding dominates.
